name: Game State Manager
description: A finite state machine for game states (menu, playing, paused, game-over) with transitions, scene loading, and persistent data. Framework-agnostic pattern for any web game.

requiredElements.name: state-machine
requiredElements.description: FSM with states (menu/playing/paused/gameover) and guarded transitions.

requiredElements.name: scene-loader
requiredElements.description: Each state has enter/exit hooks for scene setup and teardown.

requiredElements.name: transition-guard
requiredElements.description: Transitions are validated — can't go from gameover to playing without reset.

requiredElements.name: persistent-data
requiredElements.description: High score, settings, progress persist via localStorage or IndexedDB.

qualityRules.description: State transitions are explicit and guarded — no implicit state drift.
High score and settings persist across sessions.
FSM is a clean class/hook, not nested switch/if chains.
States and transitions are unit-testable in isolation.
State transitions don't accumulate event listeners or timers.

antiPatterns.description: Game state is tracked via scattered boolean flags — impossible to reason about.
Any state can transition to any other — game-over can jump back to playing without reset.
State exit doesn't dispose 3D resources — GPU memory grows each transition.
Event listeners added in enter() but not removed in exit() — duplicate handlers accumulate.

improvementSuggestions.description: Add fade transitions between states for polish.
Add mid-session save/load for longer games.
Extract FSM into dedicated class with transition table.

exampleOutputs.label: FSM transition table
